explicit_text: I was born in Thailand in 1994, where many Hmong people lived after leaving Laos. My family stayed there for some time before we moved to the United States in 2004. Thailand was a place where several Hmong families, including mine, found refuge and continued their traditions.
implicit_text: I was born in a Southeast Asian country in 1994, where many Hmong people lived after leaving Laos. My family stayed there for some time before we moved to the United States in 2004. That country was a place where several Hmong families, including mine, found refuge and continued their traditions.

explicit_text: My family and I came to the United States in 2004 after living in Thailand and Laos. I am currently a student at Century College here. The United States became our new home where we could pursue education and preserve our Hmong culture.
implicit_text: My family and I came to North America in 2004 after living in Thailand and Laos. I am currently a student at Century College here. This country became our new home where we could pursue education and preserve our Hmong culture.

explicit_text: My parents lived in Laos before moving to Thailand and eventually to the United States. They were part of the Hmong community there, and my father learned to make bamboo baskets as a child in Laos. Laos holds a special place in our family's history as the starting point of our journey.
implicit_text: My parents lived in a landlocked Southeast Asian country before moving to Thailand and eventually to the United States. They were part of the Hmong community there, and my father learned to make bamboo baskets as a child in that country. It holds a special place in our family's history as the starting point of our journey.

explicit_text: The Hmong people are my community and family. Many of us moved from Laos to Thailand and then to the United States. We have strong traditions like basket making and sewing paj ntaub, which my family practiced to support ourselves. Our culture is important, and we try to teach the younger generation about it.

We have strong traditions like basket making and sewing paj ntaub, which my family practiced to support ourselves. Our culture is important, and we try to teach the younger generation about it.

explicit_text: During the Hmong New Year, some girls dance with the bamboo baskets that are so important to our culture. This event is a time when we celebrate our traditions and show the younger generation how we use these baskets. It’s a special occasion that connects us to our heritage.
implicit_text: During the traditional New Year celebration of my ethnic group, some girls dance with the bamboo baskets that are so important to our culture. This event is a time when we celebrate our traditions and show the younger generation how we use these baskets. It’s a special occasion that connects us to our heritage.
